MEMBERS
OMER L. RAINS
CHAIRMAN
H. L. RICHARDSON
VICE CHAIRMAN
DENNIS E. CARPENTER
ALEX P. GARCIA
BILL GREENE
ARLEN GREGORIO
NATE HOLDEN

COMMITTEE ADDRESS
ROOM 5090, STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-2501

JAMES A. LHOWNING, JR.
CONSULTANT

PATRICIA JOHNSON
COMMITTEE SECRETARY

California Legislature

SENATE COMMITTEE ON
ELECTIONS AND REAPPORTIONMENT

OMER L. RAINS
CHAIRMAN

September 23, 1976

James G. Massie
District Superintendent/Principal
Washington Colony School
130 East Lincoln Avenue
Fresno, California 93706

Dear Mr. Massie:

Thank you for sending me a copy of your school board's resolution,
expressing the view that local school board members should be
excluded from the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

The Act, at Section 87300, requires agencies to adopt and
promulgate a local code of interest code. "Agency" at section 82003
includes "local government agency". "Local government agency"
includes "school district". Section 82041.

I sympathize with your view that these provisions are burdensome
on persons serving, in many cases, without any renumeration at all.
Assure, however, the drafters (and perhaps the public in voting for the initiative) perceived the real threat of
conflicts of interest in awarding contracts in the school district
as well as any other legislative or administrative body. In fact,
it is arguable, that since such a large percent of a school
board's business is awarding contracts, such boards should be included.

In any event, since the Political Reform Act was an initiative
measure, it can only be amended by a vote of the people except an
amendment "to further its purposes" could be passed by a two-thirds
vote of each house of the Legislature. A question arises as to
whether such an amendment would be construed to "further its purposes".
My staff is reviewing your suggestion and an appropriate remedy.

9 7 7
of'nitac [illegible]

James G. Massie                2                September 23, 1976


For the views of the Fair Political Practices Commission, I am
forwarding this letter to Robert Stern, general counsel.

Very truly yours,


OMER L. RAINS

OLR/jak

cc: Robert Stern, General Counsel, Fair Political Practices
    Commission, 925 J Street, Suite 522, Sacramento, California
    95814

The image appears to be a mostly blank white page with only a few small circular marks or objects visible at the top of the page. There is no readable text content to transcribe.

BOARD OF TRUSTEES
Galen Hiett, President
Eugene Sargent, Clerk
Robert Moltring
Donald Rosendahl
Arthur Jensen

James G. Massie
District Superintendent
Sally Allen
Administrative Secretary,
Bookkeeper
Eva Truesdale
Cafeteria Bookkeeper
Secretary

Washington Colony School
Phone (209) 233-0706
130 East Lincoln Avenue        Fresno, California 93706

RESOLUTION REGARDING CONFLICT OF INTEREST

WHEREAS, public school boards are required by public law (Government Code
Sections 87100 et seq.) to prepare and adopt a district Conflict
of Interest Code, and

WHEREAS, board members of said school boards are required by law to file
a disclosure statement disclosing their interest and those of
their families in investments, real property, and income, and

WHEREAS, these disclosure statements are by law made available for public
scrutiny, and

WHEREAS, other sections of the Government Code (Section 1090 et seq.) and
Title 2, California Administrative Code (Section 18700 et seq.)
and the Education Code (Section 1174) provide the needed legal
control of board member actions, and

WHEREAS, the California "Political Reform Act of 1974" is an infringement
on the rights of private citizens and their immediate families
and is an invasion of personal privacy of board members and their
immediate families, and

WHEREAS, this act can require personal expenses for attorney and accountant
fees by board members holding non-paying elective board positions,
and

WHEREAS, compliance with the requirements of this Code will encourage
private investigative snooping by persons bent on personal vendettas,
and

WHEREAS, compliance with the requirements of the California "Political Re-
form Act of 1974" by boards will result in the resignation of un-
told numbers of board members throughout the state, and

WHEREAS, school districts and communities of the State need citizens with
financial solvency, business acumen, and personal stability to serve
as board members.

IT IS HEREBY RESOLVED by the Board of Education of the Washington
Colony Elementary School District to publicly state its opposition
to the inclusion of elected public school board members within the
requirements of the California "Political Reform Act of 1974" and
it is resolved to urge its legislators to take the necessary action
to bring about the changes needed to exclude school board members
from the requirements of the California "Political Reform Act of
1974".

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY/AFFIRMATIVE ACTION EMPLOYER

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or too faint to read clearly. I can only see what appears to be some very faint marks or artifacts at the top of the page, but no legible text content is visible enough to transcribe accurately.

RESOLUTION Regarding Conflict of Interest (continued)                Page 2


Ayes:    Hiett, Sargent, Mollring, Jensen, Rosendahl

Noes:    None

Absent   None


I hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct
transcript of a resolution duly adopted and affirmed by a
formal vote of the members of the Governing Board of the
Washington Colony Elementary School District at a duly
constituted regular meeting of the Board which was open to
the public on September 7, 1976 as it appears in the minutes
of the said meeting of the said Governing Board.


                                    [signature]
                                    James G. Massie
                                    District Superintendent/Principal and
                                    Secretary to the Board

                                    WASHINGTON COLONY SCHOOL

I can see this image appears to be almost entirely blank or very faintly printed, with only a few small marks visible at the very top of the page that are too unclear to transcribe accurately.

[illegible]
